titleStmt.title: Letter from Richard F. Barry to John Gordon, 13 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Richard F. Barry

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Richard F. Barry the crown solicitor of the Kings County (Offaly) to the attorney general of Ireland John Gordon (1849-1922). Gordon became attorney general in 1915 and would leave the post shortly after this letter was written. In the letter Barry is informing Gordon that all but two suspected men are in custody after an incident in Tullamore resulted in the shooting of Sergeant Ahern. Thirteen Volunteers had been taken into custody. Due to the seriousness of the case Barry is asking for Gordon's directions of how to proceed.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/8125-10

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
John's Place,
Birr,
King's County, 13th, April, 1916. Sir:- In accordance with instructions received from the Chief
Secretary's Office, I have taken charge of the Proceedings at
present pending in connection with the shooting at the Police
in Tullamore on 20th ulto. Of the 15 Civilians who were present in the room when the
affray took place, two have not yet been arrested, and the re-
maining 13 have been again remanded in custody to Wednesday
next, as the injured Sergeant's life is not yet out of danger. Having regard to the importance of the case, I beg to ask
your permission, that the District Inspector (who was himself
present on the occasion) and I, may wait on you with a view to
reporting the steps already taken, and getting your Directions
as to those to be next taken. I have the honour to be
Sir,
Your obedient servant, Richard F.Barry
CROWN SOLICITOR. Under Secretary
Submitted
to see. WMC 18/4 I have seen Mr. Barry & the District
Inspector & given the necessary directions.
JM 18/4/16 The Right Honorable,
The Attorney General K.C. M.P.
Dublin Castle.
